title: Lithifyte guided tour
notes: selectors are for future in-app spotlight. Hosted tutorial uses the same ids via #chunk-ID anchors.

steps.title: What Lithifyte is
steps.body:
Lithifyte is household-finance mission control in a single HTML file. There is no account to create and no server that stores your bank data. You upload statements (CSV); everything is computed in your browser. Data lives in localStorage/IndexedDB on this device, and durability comes from backups you export yourself.

Privacy promise: the page does not phone home with your transactions. Optional n…
steps.location: https://lithifyte.sid-labs.com (or download index.html)

steps.title: Optional: explore the sample first
steps.body:
If you want to learn features before touching real statements, open the sample household. It is a deterministic sample household (two earners, four accounts, ~24 months of invented transactions) that exercises cashflow, budgets, wealth, the money map, and more.

The sample cannot touch data you later add to the real app. When you are ready for real data, open the main app (not /demo) and complete onboard…
steps.location: https://lithifyte.sid-labs.com/demo.html · also linked from first-run onboarding
steps.dock_hint: https://lithifyte.sid-labs.com/demo.html · also linked from first-run onboarding

steps.title: Space view — the map is the app
steps.body:
Space view is the default. The neural money map fills the screen. Sections open as glass panels over the live map (not separate pages). The left dock lists features in groups; Range and Search are top-right icons; People, Backup and Classic live under Settings.

Why it matters: first-run onboarding also runs on the map — you watch your "You" node, account, and money appear as you complete each ste…
steps.location: Default shell. Classic: 🪐 Space view pill (top-right) or button on the money map card. Exit: Settings → Switch to classic view.
steps.dock_hint: Default shell. Classic: 🪐 Space view pill (top-right) or button on the money map card. Exit: Settings → Switch to classic view.

steps.title: Where everything lives (Space dock)
steps.body:
Space dock groups:

Overview — Goals · Cashflow · Categories · Leaks
Planning — Budget · Simulate · Forecast · Plan
Wealth — Pulse · Net worth · Invest · Debts · Readiness
Data — Audit · Rules · Settings (People, Backup, Classic)

Top-right mini tools: 📅 Date range · 🔎 Search
HUD (on the map): goal progress ring · cash on hand · privacy eye · Pulse readout

Classic sticky nav: Cashflow, Categories…
steps.location: Left dock in Space view; sticky nav in Classic view
steps.dock_hint: Left dock in Space view; sticky nav in Classic view

steps.title: Step 1 — Who are you? (your name)
steps.body:
When the app has no transactions yet, a four-step welcome appears on the map.

Step 1 asks for your name (person or business). Enter it and click "That's me". A "You" node appears on the money map. This creates the first user in the household roster. You can add more people later under People / Settings.

Nothing is uploaded and nothing leaves the device at this step.
steps.location: Onboarding card over the money map (first run, zero transactions)
steps.dock_hint: Onboarding card over the money map (first run, zero transactions)

steps.title: Step 2 — Your first account
steps.body:
Name the account (e.g. "AIB current") and pick its kind:
- Current — everyday bank account; credits can be income
- Credit card — purchases are spending; payments from current are internal transfers, not income
- Savings — pot-style account; good for linking goals later

Click "Add account". The account orb joins you on the map. Kind matters forever: wrong kind distorts income and transfer detecti…
steps.location: Onboarding step 2 · later: People → accounts
steps.dock_hint: Onboarding step 2 · later: People → accounts

steps.title: Step 3 — Upload your first statement (CSV)
steps.body:
Upload a bank export as CSV. Required shape: date, description, and debit/credit (or amount) columns. Irish bank exports can be saved from Excel as CSV. Dates as YYYY-MM-DD or common bank formats work.

Duplicates are detected and skipped (safe to re-upload overlapping months). After a successful upload the dashboard unlocks: cashflow, categories, map merchants, and forecasts light up from real da…
steps.location: Onboarding step 3 · later: People → Upload a statement
steps.dock_hint: Onboarding step 3 · later: People → Upload a statement

steps.title: CSV format the app expects
steps.body:
Preferred columns: date, description, debit, credit (header row). Debit = money out, credit = money in; both non-negative. One row per transaction.

The optional feeder CLI (feeder/feeder.mjs) pulls banks via GoCardless PSD2 and writes exactly this CSV shape. Re-uploads are safe because of multiset dedupe per account.
steps.location: Upload form hint · feeder writes this format
steps.dock_hint: Upload form hint · feeder writes this format

steps.title: Step 4 — Your first goal
steps.body:
Name a goal (e.g. House deposit), set a euro target, and optionally a deadline month. This becomes your primary goal. The map grows a gold goal orb with a progress ring; the HUD shows progress.

Goals are funded from cautious forecast surplus in priority order (waterfall). You can add more goals, reorder, set confirmed pots, or link a goal to a real savings account later.
steps.location: Onboarding step 4 · later: Goals panel / goal hero
steps.dock_hint: Onboarding step 4 · later: Goals panel / goal hero

steps.title: The money map — explore your household
steps.body:
The map shows people → accounts → category hubs → merchants, plus goals (gold ring), holdings, assets, and hollow debt orbs.

Controls: click a hub to dive in (merchants appear); double-click to fold; scroll to zoom; drag background to pan; click any node for a story card with shortcuts. ▶ Play history time-lapses month by month.

Shared merchants (both partners pay Netflix) are one node with two …
steps.location: Space stage (always) · Classic: Money map section · dock does not list map separately in space because it IS the background
steps.dock_hint: Space stage (always) · Classic: Money map section · dock does not list map separately in space because it IS the background

steps.title: Date range and Search
steps.body: Date range scopes charts, leaks, categories, and much of the map flow. Presets and custom from/to are available. Search finds transactions by merchant (raw or canonical name) and can highlight related map nodes. Use Search when a number looks wrong and you need the underlying rows.
steps.location: Space: top-right 📅 and 🔎 icons · Classic: range bar under header + Search section
steps.dock_hint: Space: top-right 📅 and 🔎 icons · Classic: range bar under header + Search section

steps.title: Cash on hand and privacy mode
steps.body: Cash on hand is reconciled non-credit account balances (needs opening balances for full accuracy). Tap the cash figure to mask it; press-and-hold peeks while masked. The 👁 button toggles app-wide privacy mode: every euro amount renders as € ••• (dates and percentages stay readable). When the at-rest lock is on, masking defaults to on. Privacy prefs ride in backups.
steps.location: Space HUD + classic goal hero · eye buttons
steps.dock_hint: Space HUD + classic goal hero · eye buttons

steps.title: People, accounts & more statements
steps.body:
Add more household members, accounts, and upload further CSVs under the right account. Account kinds (current / credit / savings) change how credits count. You can rename people/accounts and delete user-added accounts (with consequences listed).

Always file each statement under the correct account so transfer matching and reconciliation work.
steps.location: Space: Settings (🛠️) includes People · Classic: People section · sec-people
steps.dock_hint: Space: Settings (🛠️) includes People · Classic: People section · sec-people

steps.title: Opening balances and reconciliation
steps.body: Set an account's opening balance and as-of date so Lithifyte can roll forward from transactions and compare to a checked real balance. Drift ≥ €1 is flagged (possible missing months or miscategorised transfers). Reconciled cash powers cash-on-hand, net worth, and liquidity in Pulse. Linked goals use the linked account's reconciled balance as the pot.
steps.location: People → each account card (opening balance + as-of + checked balance)
steps.dock_hint: People → each account card (opening balance + as-of + checked balance)

steps.title: Internal transfers between your accounts
steps.body:
Equal-and-opposite amounts on different accounts within a few days are scored as possible internal transfers (card payments, savings standing orders, top-ups). High-confidence pairs auto-apply as Internal transfer (excluded from income/spending/forecast). Weaker pairs wait for your yes/no ruling.

Special case: if one side is Deposit savings, that side still counts toward goals — only the mirror l…
steps.location: People section → "Between your own accounts" card (when candidates exist)
steps.dock_hint: People section → "Between your own accounts" card (when candidates exist)

steps.title: Why re-uploading is safe (dedupe)
steps.body: Each row is fingerprinted date|merchant|debit|credit and compared as a multiset per account. Two identical bus fares on one day can both be real; if the file has three and the account already has two, exactly one is added. Whole-file near-duplicates ask for confirmation. Re-pulling feeder CSVs will not double-count.
steps.location: Automatic on every statement upload
steps.dock_hint: Automatic on every statement upload

steps.title: Audit — auto-categorisation review
steps.body: Lists merchant → category decisions (biggest money first) with confidence. Filter by AI-assigned vs your rules, by category, or search. Hit Fix on a wrong row to pre-fill a correction rule. Statement name variants (ALDI 887, ALDI51873) group under one merchant; open "N names" to split or review merges. Your rules always beat the feed's guesses.
steps.location: Space dock: Data → Audit · Classic: Audit section
steps.dock_hint: Space dock: Data → Audit · Classic: Audit section

steps.title: Rules — fix categories permanently
steps.body:
First-match-wins rules: merchant contains (substring) plus optional amount equals/from/to. Reorder with ▲▼. Narrow rules above broad ones. Special categories: Deposit savings (counts toward goals, not spending) and Internal transfer (owned by the transfer matcher — do not force via rules casually).

Every rule should have a human label. Disabled rules stay as history. After changes, re-check Audit…
steps.location: Space dock: Data → Rules · Classic: Rules section
steps.dock_hint: Space dock: Data → Rules · Classic: Rules section

steps.title: Merchant names merged (canonicalisation)
steps.body: Banks mangle merchant strings (store numbers, SEPA tails, card refs). Lithifyte groups display names under a canonical prefix for the map, audit, and search — raw strings remain the identity for rules, recurring, transfers, and dedupe. You can force-merge or exempt names. One rule with a stable token (e.g. PROVINCEF) can fix a whole group.
steps.location: Audit card (merge/split) · map merchant nodes
steps.dock_hint: Audit card (merge/split) · map merchant nodes

steps.title: AI categoriser (optional, local)
steps.body:
Point the card at an OpenAI-compatible server on your machine (Ollama, LM Studio; Ollama needs CORS origins configured). It proposes categories for uncategorised / low-confidence merchants — nothing auto-applies. Accepting creates a normal front-inserted rule you can undo. Savings, rent, and gambling-shaped calls are flagged for your judgement.

Hosted pages often cannot call localhost (browser ru…
steps.location: Audit section → "AI categoriser — your local model" card
steps.dock_hint: Audit section → "AI categoriser — your local model" card

steps.title: Cashflow — month by month
steps.body: Bar chart of income vs spending (deposit savings excluded from spending) for the selected range. Hover a month for totals; click a month for a full Income / Expenses / Savings breakdown with clickable categories → transactions. Use this first when a month looks weird.
steps.location: Space: Overview → Cashflow · Classic: Cashflow
steps.dock_hint: Space: Overview → Cashflow · Classic: Cashflow

steps.title: Money flow (Sankey)
steps.body: Sankey diagram: income sources → money-in hub → savings, top spending categories, leftovers. Balanced to the cent: overspend shows as "From balance", not hidden. Follows the cashflow selected month or range averages. Privacy mode masks euro labels. Click the card or ⛶ for full-size overlay.
steps.location: Cashflow section → money flow card (click for large view)
steps.dock_hint: Cashflow section → money flow card (click for large view)

steps.title: Categories — where a month goes
steps.body: Average monthly outgoings by category in the selected range. Click a bar to list every transaction behind it. Colours follow your category settings. This is the bridge between raw merchants and budget lines.
steps.location: Space: Overview → Categories · Classic: Categories
steps.dock_hint: Space: Overview → Categories · Classic: Categories

steps.title: Leaks — where money dribbles away
steps.body: Ranks recurring or high-frequency spend drains (subscriptions, habits) with monthly averages. Start here for quick wins before fine-tuning budgets. Pair with Rules if a "leak" is miscategorised.
steps.location: Space: Overview → Leaks · Classic: Leaks
steps.dock_hint: Space: Overview → Leaks · Classic: Leaks

steps.title: Goals — feasibility and pots
steps.body:
Goals are ordered pockets. Each forecast month's cautious surplus fills them top-down. Cards show progress, required €/mo, and verdicts (on track / tight / at risk) with cautious date first ("Mar 2027, or Dec 2026 if trends hold").

Pot sources (highest wins): (1) linked account reconciled balance, (2) confirmed pot + as-of plus later Deposit savings, (3) inferred cumulative Deposit savings. Prote…
steps.location: Space: Overview → Goals · Classic: goal hero at top
steps.dock_hint: Space: Overview → Goals · Classic: goal hero at top

steps.title: Link a goal to a savings account
steps.body: Best practice for a credit-union or savings pot: add the savings account, set opening balance, upload its statements (or transfers into it), then link the goal to that account. The pot becomes the account's reconciled balance (with transfer top-ups when needed). Stronger than a typed pot number.
steps.location: Goals editor · accountId on a goal · People opening balance on that account
steps.dock_hint: Goals editor · accountId on a goal · People opening balance on that account

steps.title: Budget — spending pace
steps.body: Per-category monthly budgets plus "Everything else". Seed with 📊 match my history (median of last complete months) or 🎯 align to my goal (trim variable spend so income − goal savings − buffer fits). Pace is recurring-aware: rent on day 1 does not false-alarm; variable spend tracks the calendar. See spent, pace bar, projected month-end, safe-to-spend €/day, and hit/miss dots. Breaches feed the visi…
steps.location: Space: Planning → Budget · Classic: Budget
steps.dock_hint: Space: Planning → Budget · Classic: Budget

steps.title: Forecast and recurring costs
steps.body: Detects monthly and periodic bills from history (with confidence and yearly-alignment guards). You can end items, add manual future costs, and set buffer %. Two paths: expected vs cautious (buffer from robust σ of irregular spend; spike months named). Goal verdicts use the cautious path. Debts can clamp a recurring item's end to payoff so surplus releases automatically.
steps.location: Space: Planning → Forecast · Classic: Forecast
steps.dock_hint: Space: Planning → Forecast · Classic: Forecast

steps.title: Scenario simulator
steps.body: Sliders on top discretionary categories (elasticity-based max cuts). Labels show −€cut → €left/mo. Cuts feed the forecast so goal dates update live. Add fixed categories as levers or exclude noisy ones via chips. Use after leaks/budget to test "what if we cut dining €100?".
steps.location: Space: Planning → Simulate · Classic: Simulator
steps.dock_hint: Space: Planning → Simulate · Classic: Simulator

steps.title: Wealth plan cards (live narrative)
steps.body: Ordered action cards computed from your data (savings pattern, Help to Buy estimate, grocery shifts, hottest leaks, bill switches, readiness flags). Worth figures are per year against actual spend. They rewrite when you recategorise or upload. Not static advice copy.
steps.location: Space: Planning → Plan · Classic: Plan
steps.dock_hint: Space: Planning → Plan · Classic: Plan

steps.title: Financial pulse
steps.body: 0–100 dial over up to 7 absolute measures (spend vs income, bill reliability, liquidity months, long-term share, debt service, budget pace, streak+goal). Zones from Critical to Thriving. Window toggle Month/Quarter/Year/Overall scopes flow measures; balances are as-of today. Unscored measures explain why (e.g. no opening balance → liquidity dark). Best next move is ranked for you.
steps.location: Space: Wealth → Pulse (also HUD) · Classic: Wealth → Financial pulse
steps.dock_hint: Space: Wealth → Pulse (also HUD) · Classic: Wealth → Financial pulse

steps.title: Net worth
steps.body: Composes reconciled cash + goal pots not already inside that cash + holdings + manual assets − debts − credit balances. Stale valuations amber; possible double-counts flagged. Add property/vehicle/pension etc. with as-of dates. Daily trend sparkline logs one point per open day.
steps.location: Space: Wealth → Net worth · Classic: Wealth section
steps.dock_hint: Space: Wealth → Net worth · Classic: Wealth section

steps.title: Loans and debt
steps.body: Track balances with APR and payment or end date — amortisation derives the rest. Link a statement merchant so the forecast ends that bill at payoff instead of double-counting. Overpay what-ifs and avalanche vs snowball when multiple debts. Feeds readiness commitments and net worth. Nudges appear for loan-shaped recurring items not yet tracked.
steps.location: Space: Wealth → Debts · Classic: Wealth → Loans & debt
steps.dock_hint: Space: Wealth → Debts · Classic: Wealth → Loans & debt

steps.title: Investments and crypto
steps.body: Positions with units, cost, kind (share/ETF/fund/crypto/pension). Quotes: manual, ↻ crypto via CoinGecko when opened as a local file, or 📥 import quotes JSON from the feeder. Irish tax layer: CGT vs fund exit tax, exemption, 8-year deemed disposal on funds. Stale quotes flagged. Values feed net worth.
steps.location: Space: Wealth → Invest · Classic: Wealth → Investments & crypto
steps.dock_hint: Space: Wealth → Invest · Classic: Wealth → Investments & crypto

steps.title: Irish schemes, pension AVC, post-goal reallocation
steps.body: Estimates Help to Buy headroom, idle-pot cost, pension AVC leverage (age bands, relief) with honest goal-delay trade-off, and where surplus might go after goals (pension vs overpay vs funds after exit tax vs cash). Fiscal constants live in a dated block — verify on revenue.ie before acting. Figures are planning estimates, not advice.
steps.location: Wealth section cards: schemes, pension, reallocation
steps.dock_hint: Wealth section cards: schemes, pension, reallocation

steps.title: Mortgage readiness checklist
steps.body: Live checks lenders care about over recent months: savings streak, gambling-strong vs ambiguous merchants (you must rule on ambiguous CAS/BET-style names — the app will not whitewash), uncategorised %, missed payments, credit commitments/DSR, overdraft low-point (needs balances). Fail-first ordering. Aim for clean months before applying.
steps.location: Space: Wealth → Readiness · Classic: Readiness
steps.dock_hint: Space: Wealth → Readiness · Classic: Readiness

steps.title: Backup, restore, encryption, lock
steps.body: Export finance-backup-*.json with rules, goals, uploads, budgets, debts, holdings, etc. Optional passphrase → AES-GCM encrypted file (PBKDF2 ~310k). Import restores on a new browser/device. Opt-in at-rest lock seals all dd-* stores until passphrase on open — no recovery if forgotten. Erase-all (danger zone) double-confirms and wipes browser state for this app.
steps.location: Space: Settings → Backup · Classic: Backup section (Keep this dashboard alive)
steps.dock_hint: Space: Settings → Backup · Classic: Backup section (Keep this dashboard alive)

steps.title: Integrity and self-test
steps.body: Integrity checks embedded transaction checksums. Self-test runs ~100 behavioural checks on load — must be green before trusting numbers after an update. Render errors name the broken section. If self-test is red, something changed; do not rely on figures until fixed.
steps.location: Backup section · footer status
steps.dock_hint: Backup section · footer status

steps.title: Feeder CLI (optional bank sync and quotes)
steps.body: Node CLI: banks / connect / fetch write CSVs for upload; prices SYM writes EUR quotes JSON for import. Secrets stay in feeder/config.json (gitignored). Bank credentials never enter the HTML file. GoCardless signup availability can block live bank tests; CSV upload always works. See feeder/README.md.…
steps.location: Repo folder feeder/ — runs on your machine, not in the browser

steps.title: Classic view vs Space view
steps.body: Classic: all sections on one page, sticky nav — better for screen readers, keyboard, printing, or if glass panels feel heavy. Space: immersive map-first. Same data, same engines. Choose what you prefer; stored in settings.
steps.location: Settings → Switch to classic view · 🪐 to return
steps.dock_hint: Settings → Switch to classic view · 🪐 to return

steps.title: Ask — questions about the app (RAG + local LM)
steps.body:
Ask answers questions about how Lithifyte works using a local RAG corpus (the tutorial/feature chunks), not your bank transactions. Retrieval is client-side over docs/rag/chunks.json. When a local OpenAI-compatible model is configured, the top chunks become context for a short explanatory answer — still assist-only, still on-device unless you point it at your own gateway.

Ask will refuse to inven…
steps.location: Tutorial page Ask panel · future in-app Ask

steps.title: Recommended order of use (checklist)
steps.body:
Recommended path for a new household:

1) Optional sample → 2) Name → 3) First account (correct kind) → 4) First CSV → 5) First goal → 6) Explore map/HUD → 7) Add other people/accounts/statements → 8) Opening balances → 9) Resolve transfer rulings → 10) Audit + rules (and optional AI categoriser) → 11) Cashflow / categories / leaks → 12) Budget seed → 13) Forecast buffer check → 14) Simulator what-i…
steps.location: Whole app
